metadata.raw: {"ID":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/id/nia/2025/5","TITLE":"Assembly Members (Independent Remuneration Board) Act (Northern Ireland) 2025","LINK (SELF)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/id/nia/2025/5","LINK":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/nia/2025/5/enacted","LINK (XML)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/nia/2025/5/enacted/data.xml","LINK (RDF/XML)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/nia/2025/5/enacted/data.rdf","LINK (AKN)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/nia/2025/5/enacted/data.akn","LINK (HTML snippet)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/nia/2025/5/enacted/data.xht","LINK (Website (XHTML) Default View)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/nia/2025/5/enacted/data.htm","LINK (PDF)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/nia/2025/5/enacted/data.pdf","LINK (Table of Contents)":"http://www.legislation.gov.uk/nia/2025/5/contents/enacted","AUTHOR":"","UPDATED":"2025-11-27T09:24:33Z","PUBLISHED":"2025-09-23T13:22:52.344312Z","DocumentMainType":"NorthernIrelandAct","YEAR":"2025","NUMBER":"5","ISBN":"'9780105962908'","CreationDate":"2025-09-16","SUMMARY":"Amend the Assembly Members (Independent Financial Review and Standards) Act (Northern Ireland) 2011 to make provision about the name, functions and membership of the Independent Financial Review Panel; and for connected purposes.","":""}

content: ction 12(3) of the 2011 Act (double jobbing) for “either House of Parliament” substitute “the House of Lords” . (3) The following have effect— (a) in sections 1(1) and 15 of the 2011 Act, and the heading of Part 1, for “Independent Financial Review Panel” substitute “Independent Remuneration Board” ;  (b) for the word “Panel” in the 2011 Act, in each place other than Schedule 5 (including headings but excluding the long title), substitute “Board” ;  (c) for the word “Panel’s” in the 2011 Act, in each place, substitute “Board’s” ;  (d) in the following provisions, for “The Independent Financial Review Panel” substitute “The Independent Remuneration Board” —  (i) Schedule 3 to the Public Services Ombudsman Act (Northern Ireland) 2016; and  (ii) Part 7 of Schedule 1 to the Freedom of Information Act 2000 (public bodies and offices: Northern Ireland).  Supplementary provisions Continuity of the law 8. —(1) Subsection (2) applies to any determination (“the original determination”) made under— (a) section 47 or 48 of the 1998 Act (including a determination made in exercise of powers conferred by resolution of the Assembly);  (b) any provision of the 2011 Act;  in respect of salaries, allowances, pensions or gratuities for, or in respect of, members or former members of the Assembly. (2) The original determination shall continue to have effect in accordance with its terms except to the extent (if any) that it— (a) has expired (expressly or by implication); or  (b) has been revoked or superseded (expressly or by implication) by a new determination under the provisions referred to in subsection (1);  whether or not the original determination could have been made under the provisions of the 2011 Act as amended by this Act. (3) In any enactment, instrument or other document a reference to “the Independent Financial Review Panel” under section 1 of the 2011 Act shall be construed as being or including a reference to the Panel by its new title “the Independent Remuneration Board” if and in so far as necessary for the purposes of its continuing to have effect in accordance with its terms. Interpretation 9. In this Act— “ the 1998 Act ” means the Northern Ireland Act 1998; and “ the 2011 Act ” means the Assembly Members (Independent Financial Review and Standards) Act (Northern Ireland) 2011. Commencement 10. This Act comes into operation on the day after the day on which it receives Royal Assent. Short title 11. This Act may be cited as the Assembly Members (Independent Remuneration Board) Act (Northern Ireland) 2025. Previous Next Back to top Options/Help Print Options Official printed copies Order a copy of this legislation buy now Print The Whole Act PDF The Whole Act Web page The Whole Act Legislation is available in different versions: Latest Available (revised): The latest available updated version of the legislation incorporating changes made by subsequent legislation and applied by our editorial team. Changes we have not yet applied to the text, can be found in the ‘Changes to Legislation’ area.
